description: Brief-first music video workflow that generates a composition bundle and muxes a music track through the rendered video boundary.

params.content:
# Music Video Brief

- Audience: Kyberion operators and maintainers
- Objective: Show that a music artifact can be treated as a governed audio reference and muxed into a deterministic video bundle.
- Content type: music_video
- Presentation mode: promo
- Promise: The same video composition boundary can render a music-driven video without a shell-heavy render script.
- Desired takeaway: Audio generation and video rendering stay separated, but the final mux step is still governed.
- Constraints: Keep the scene plan simple, keep audio selection explicit, and verify the final artifact with audio and video streams present.

video_composition_adf.intent: music-driven video from a governed audio reference
video_composition_adf.title: Kyberion Music Video Demo

content.headline: Music-driven video, no shell branching
content.body: The music track is selected explicitly and carried through the governed render boundary.

content.headline: Audio stays separate
content.body: The music generator and the video renderer keep their own contracts, then mux at the end.

content.headline: Governed muxing
content.body: The final artifact still passes through the same video verification step.
